ogTitle: Hiamdale vs High Camp: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hiamdale and High Camp.
heading: Hiamdale vs High Camp — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hiamdale, Victoria and High Camp, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hiamdale has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to High Camp (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hiamdale or High Camp safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hiamdale has a flood risk score of 39/100, while High Camp scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hiamdale scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. High Camp scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hiamdale or High Camp?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hiamdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
